title: ledebouria viscosa
intro: Ledebouria viscosa, also known as sticky-leaved ledebouria or sticky-leaved onion, is a species of plant in the family Asparagaceae, native to South Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for wound healing and fighting off infections. Herbal preparations of Ledebouria viscosa can be made by infusing its roots or leaves in water, which can be used as a tea or applied topically to affected areas.
